items.title: Japan May Move to Managing Yields, Not Yen, Deutsche Bank Says
items.description: Japan may have to shift its focus from supporting the yen toward controlling government bond yields if it is to achieve its ambitious economic growth plans, according to Deutsche Bank AG.

items.title: BofA Equities Traders Say Buy Momentum Stocks, Eyeing 5% Gain
items.description: Bank of America Corp.’s trading desk is urging clients to buy US momentum stocks, betting that the group has reached an attractive price level after a bout of profit taking.

items.title: Wall Street Won’t Give Up on Saylor’s Strategy, Sees 170% Upside
items.description: Michael Saylor’s Strategy Inc. is confronting a collapse of confidence almost everywhere — except among Wall Street analysts.

items.title: Aston Martin Raises £550 Million Debt From HPS to Boost Cash
items.description: Aston Martin Lagonda Global Holdings Plc has struck a deal for £550 million ($736 million) of fresh funds with BlackRock Inc.-owned HPS Investment Partners to bolster its liquidity.

items.title: The AI Export Boom That Wasn’t
items.description: Thailand’s tech exports are booming but so is its trade deficit.

items.title: Heat and War Deal Europe’s Grain Harvest Biggest Blow in Decades
items.description: Europe is set to suffer one of its sharpest ever declines in grain production as extreme weather and geopolitical tensions converge to pile pressure on farmers.

items.title: EasyJet Shares Slump After Report on EU Ownership Rules Review
items.description: EasyJet Plc shares fell the most in four years on Wednesday after Reuters reported that the European Union is preparing to review airline ownership rules that it said might affect takeover bids for th

items.title: Zambia’s World-Beating 36% Local Bond Rally to Extend After Polls, Citi Says
items.description: The world-beating rally in Zambia’s bonds is likely to extend if President Hakainde Hichilema decisively wins next month’s elections, according to Citigroup Inc.

items.title: Galaxy Digital Seeks $3.5 Billion of Debt for Texas Data Center
items.description: Galaxy Digital Inc. is planning to raise about $3.5 billion from a debut junk-bond sale to fund a data center tied to CoreWeave Inc., extending AI financing’s push into the riskier corners of the US c

items.title: GE Vernova Falls as Outlook Clashes With ‘Too High’ AI Hopes
items.description: GE Vernova Inc.’s shares slid after a higher revenue outlook failed to impress investors, a sign that it’s getting harder for companies that sell equipment to data centers to meet the market’s sky-hig

items.title: Vale Elects Ollie Chairman After Weeks of Turmoil
items.description: Vale SA shareholders elected Manuel Lino Silva de Sousa Oliveira as chairman on Wednesday, concluding weeks of boardroom turmoil sparked by an effort from one of the Brazilian mining giant’s largest i

items.title: S&P 500 Fluctuates as Traders Brace for Alphabet, Tesla Results
items.description: US stocks swung between small gains and losses after chipmakers came off session lows and oil prices jumped on the escalation of the US-Iran war. Traders are also bracing for key earnings from Alphabe

items.title: Stumbling Stock Rally Looks to Earnings for Second Half Boost
items.description: Investors who spent weeks watching US stocks go nowhere are pinning their hopes for a breakout on a key pillar of the four-year bull run: corporate earnings.

items.title: Prologis Wins Segro Backing for £14 Billion Takeover
items.description: Prologis Inc. is set to acquire the UK’s largest landlord Segro Plc after securing the recommendation of its board for an improved £14 billion ($18.7 billion) cash and share offer for the company.

items.title: Kuwait Is Set to Sell $6 Billion Bond Even as Iran Strikes
items.description: Kuwait is set to raise $6 billion from a three-part dollar bond sale on Wednesday, highlighting resilient demand for the OPEC member’s debt even as it comes under daily missile and drone attacks from

items.title: Houthi Threats Give Oil Markets a ‘Two Chokepoint Problem’
items.description: Ships continue to turn away from the southern end of the Red Sea, after Yemen’s Iran-backed Houthi rebels announced an embargo on Saudi exports through the Bab el-Mandeb Strait, a narrow channel that

items.title: Gold Advances as Dip-Buying Outweighs War, Inflation Worries
items.description: Gold extended gains, supported by dip-buyers who scooped up the metal after prices have drifted lower in recent weeks on concerns that US inflationary pressure will prompt the Federal Reserve to raise

items.title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 22
items.description: Oil briefly spiked above $95, extending this month’s price jump to around 30%, as the US and Iran played down the prospect of peace talks and disruptions to global supplies continued to mount.

items.title: Stocks Churn Before Big-Tech Earnings as Oil Jumps: Markets Wrap
items.description: Wall Street investors bracing for the start of the megacap earnings season sent stocks wavering while worries about an escalation of the Iran war boosted oil prices.

items.title: This ETF Tax Loophole Is All the Rage Among Rich Americans
items.description: A bespoke fund is becoming a must-have tool for wealthy investors looking to defer — and sometimes even avoid — capital gains tax.
